are: verb. second-person singular simple present of be
arse: noun. A person's buttocks; the bottom, the backside.
ass: noun. Any of several species of horse-like animals, especially Equus asinus, the domesticated of which are used as beasts of burden.
assure: verb. To make sure and secure; ensure.
ear: noun. The organ of hearing, consisting of the pinna or auricle, auditory canal, eardrum, malleus, incus, stapes and cochlea.
ease: noun. Lack of difficulty; the ability to do something easily.
era: noun. A time period of indeterminate length, generally more than one year.
erases: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of erase
erasure: noun. The action of erasing; deletion; obliteration.
errs: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of err
ers: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of er
ess: noun. The name of the Latin script letter S / s.
rares: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of rare
ras: noun. An Ethiopian king or prince.
rase: verb. To be extremely angry; to rage; specifically, of a dog or wolf: to snarl in rage.
raser: noun. Alternative form of razer (“one who razes”).
rasure: noun. Scraping the surface of a parchment etc. in order to erase something from the document; erasure, more generally.
rear: verb. To bring up to maturity, as offspring; to educate; to instruct; to foster.
reassure: verb. To assure anew; to restore confidence to; to free from fear or self-doubt.
ree: noun. Alternative form of rei (“Portuguese real”).
reuse: noun. The act of salvaging or in some manner restoring a discarded item to yield something usable.
reuses: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of reuse
ruse: noun. A turning or doubling back, especially of animals to get out of the way of hunting dogs.
sae: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sabanê.
sau: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Saleman.
sea: noun. A large body of salt water.
sear: adjective. Dry; withered, especially of vegetation.
searer: noun. A person or machine that sears food.
sears: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of sear
see: verb. To perceive or detect someone or something with the eyes, or as if by sight.
seer: noun. One who foretells the future; a clairvoyant, prophet, soothsayer or diviner.
sees: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of see
ser: noun. An old Indian unit of weight, equal to 80 tolas, or one fortieth of a maund.
sera: noun. plural of serum; alternative form of serums
serer: adjective. comparative form of sere: more sere
sue: verb. To file a legal action against someone, generally a non-criminal (civil) action.
suer: noun. Someone who sues; a suitor.
sues: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of sue
sura: noun. Any of the 114 chapters of the Quran.
sure: adjective. Physically secure and certain, non-failing, reliable.
surer: adjective. comparative form of sure: more sure
surra: noun. A disease of vertebrate animals caused by protozoan trypanosomes, involving fever, weakness, and lethargy.
urea: noun. A water-soluble organic compound, CO(NH 2) 2, formed by the metabolism of proteins and excreted in the urine.
urease: noun. the enzyme, found in soil bacteria and some plants, that catalyzes the hydrolysis of urea into ammonia and carbon dioxide
user: noun. One who uses or makes use of something, a consumer or client or an express or implied licensee (free user) or a trespasser.
aes: noun. plural of a, the name of the letter A.
aru: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Arua.
ase: noun. An essential energy in West African religions.
easer: noun. A person or thing that eases or relieves
rasse: noun. Viverricula indica, the small Indian civet.
serra: noun. A saw, or saw-like part.
ssu: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Susuami.
sur: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mwaghavul.
sus: noun. Suspicion (in terms of a sus law).
ures: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of ure
urs: noun. The anniversary of the death of a Sufi saint, celebrated with music and ritual.
usee: noun. One who or that which is used.
